seo.title: Stuffed Peppers - Lovely with a Salad - Marinda Kook

html:
Stuffed peppers or tomatoes take me back to my childhood. My mother always made it for us on a Sunday evening. What you use as filling is whatever you like. I used mince because we ate it as a main meal with salad.

YOU NEED:

  • 4 red/green or yellow peppers
  • 600 g mince
  • 1 small onion, finely chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
  • 1 tomato, cut into small cubes
  • 125 ml cooked rice
  • 20 ml Worcestershire sauce
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • 60 ml chopped parsley
  • 20 ml Chutney
  • 1 chilli, finely chopped (leave out if you don't like heat)
  • 250 ml grated cheese

 

NOW:

  1. set the oven to 180 degrees Celsius
  2. cut the tops off the peppers, remove the seeds and wash thoroughly under cold water
  3. mix together all the rest of the ingredients (only mix in half the cheese) and fill the peppers with the mixture
  4. place in an oven dish and bake covered with foil for 30 minutes
  5. remove the foil, put the remaining cheese on the meat and bake uncovered for another 30 minutes

Pour yourself a glass of wine and enjoy the delicious peppers with a green salad and bread.
